_description: AH-gate Stage 1 known counter deltas. The counter gate (gate=counters) compares per-stage architectural-invariant counts (dpe_fire_count, pass_count, row_count, lane_count, softmax_exp_fires, softmax_norm_fires) — Fmax-independent integers describing the same hardware. A delta entry here documents a real disagreement with file:line root-cause citations; the gate accepts the residual if |delta - delta_count| <= tolerance.

_schema.key: dpe_fire_count | pass_count | row_count | lane_count | softmax_exp_fires | softmax_norm_fires
_schema.delta_count: RTL - sim (negative = RTL fires fewer times than sim expects; positive = RTL fires more)
_schema.tolerance: allowed wiggle around delta_count (typically 0 — counter equality is integer)
_schema.root_cause: file:line citation of the structural or modelling source of the residual

_status_2026_04_25_post_bug_3: STAGE 1 + Bug-1 + Bug-2 + Bug-3 fixes applied. Bug 3 fix (this commit): sim counter export now multiplied by physical fan-out factors (lane × identity × byte) to match RTL fire convention. Specifically (a) gemm_log gained a K_id_phys parameter (counter-only; latency/energy unchanged) so NL-DPE DIMM mac_qk uses K_id_phys=2 (RTL c=128 dual_identity) and mac_sv uses K_id_phys=1 (no S×V dual at this size); (b) gemm_log also gained helper_fires_per_row_per_lane to add NL-DPE wsum's ws_log scalar fire (=1 per Q-row per W lane); (c) gemm_dsp gained fires_per_lane_per_row (counter-only) so DIMM mac_qk's redundant-lane topology (each of W=16 lanes scans N K-rows per Q-row → N fires per lane per row) is correctly counted as M×N×W; (d) scheduler._run_dimm now passes n_parallel_outputs_dimm=W (= total_dimm_dpes=16) instead of N (was placeholder); (e) scheduler._run_linear (AL branch) now emits per-DSP fires (M×N=8192 per arm × 3 arms = 24576) instead of imc_core.run_gemm's IMC-tile fires (M=128 → 384 with implicit 3-arm aggregation); (f) scheduler._record_softmax_counters now scales exp/norm per-lane fires by W lanes; AL exp specifically uses rows×cols (per-cycle valid input strobes) since clb_softmax fires every S_LOAD cycle. All Bug-3 deltas collapsed to ±1 except AL mac_sv +32 (FSM transition edge effect — rising edges captured between S_DIMM_WAIT advance decision and dimm_soft_rst settling, see entry below).

deltas.root_cause: fc_verification/rtl/azurelily_attn_head_d64_c128.v:300-334 (S_DIMM_WAIT → S_DIMM_RST). Head FSM advances on first dimm_valid_n_w pulse, but DIMM_RST_CYCLES=4 (line 222) holds the soft-reset for 4 cycles before re-entering S_DIMM_FIRE. During the transition window (= the 1 cycle from advance decision until dimm_soft_rst settles for all 16 lanes via dimm_rst_internal = rst | dimm_soft_rst on line 184), lanes that were 1 K=32 cycle behind fire their valid_n right at the boundary. Aggregated across 128 Q-rows, this yields ~32 extra mac_sv valid_n rising edges (~0.25 per Q-row, summed across 16 lanes — matches edge-cycle FSM transition timing). Pure per-Q-row per-lane count is 1, so RTL ≈ M*W=2048 + ~16-32 boundary spillover = 2080. Sim's gemm_dsp models the FSM-cutoff as exactly M*W=2048 (no boundary spill). Classification: modelling_granularity.
Same as mac_sv.dpe_fire_count (FSM transition edge effect, pass_count is an alias).
